source.description: UFCStats-derived fight-level dataset containing date, participants, result, and division.
source.sourceLicense: MIT repository; UFCStats is the underlying statistical source.

methodology.purpose: Measure competitive depth within each UFC division over time without using a generic old-versus-new penalty.
methodology.activePoolDefinition: At least two UFC appearances through the snapshot and a fight in the division during the prior 730 days.

qualifiedActivePool.description: Number of qualified active fighters in the division.

ranksSixToFifteenElo.description: Mean division-specific Elo strength for ranks 6–15, with empty slots filled at replacement Elo.

contenderDiversity.description: Unique fighters appearing in the top 15 over the trailing 12 quarterly snapshots.

methodology.baseline: Each division is normalized to its own most recent 6-year mature window; sparse divisions use their most recent valid snapshots. This isolates era depth from the app's separate division-strength treatment.
methodology.fighterSampling: Every UFC fight inside the app’s canonical prime window is sampled at the division’s nearest prior quarterly depth snapshot. Title fights receive 1.5× weight. Open primes receive one terminal modern snapshot.
caveats: The external fight dataset may end before the app’s latest 2026 results; open primes therefore receive the latest available modern snapshot.
Elo is a depth proxy, not a claim that athletes from one era were inherently more skilled.
The output is shadow-only and must not mutate live category scores, ranks, or OVRs.

curvedCandidate.purpose: Translate the empirical depth index without flattening every moderately older era at the same maximum penalty.
curvedCandidate.interpretation: A 0.75 index receives the full -3; a moderate 0.89 index receives about -0.9 instead of the same -3.
